content.text:
A 24 is a solid hit against most yugoloths! Let's see what you're dealing with:

- **Mezzoloth** (AC 18) — a 24 hits
- **Dhergoloth** (AC 15) — hits easily
- **Nycaloth** (AC 18) — hits
- **Arcanaloth** (AC 17) — hits

So unless you've stumbled onto something nastier (an **Ultroloth** sits at AC 19, still a hit), your +1 Rapier finds its mark. 🗡️

Go ahead and **roll your damage** — 1d8 piercing +1 from the rapier, plus your Dexterity modifier (and Sneak Attack if you have advantage or an ally adjacent, plus any Rage bonus if you're raging as your Barbarian).

Quick question before you roll the dice: are you **raging** right now? Because if so, that affects whether you can use spells/concentration, and it stacks nice damage on that piercing hit. And do you have a buddy next to your target for **Sneak Attack**?

What does the damage come to?
